message:
perf(schema): parse CREATE TABLE once and reuse the AST during schema reload

When Connection rebuilds its in-memory schema from sqlite_master
(e.g. on open, on schema-version change), every row used to drive
four to five independent calls that each re-parsed the same
create_sql string:

- parse_columns_from_sqlite_master_sql (AST or fallback regex)
- is_without_rowid_table_sql (tokenizer)
- is_autoincrement_table_sql (AST or tokenizer)
- extract_check_constraints_from_sql (AST or tokenizer)
- is_strict_table_sql (tokenizer)
- the Statement::CreateTable discriminant match for FK + UNIQUE
  constraint harvesting

For a database with N tables, that's up to 6N parser passes over
strings that are already in the row buffer. parse_single_statement
is not free — it owns the full SQL lexer + parser — and on the
open-many-tables workload it dominates the cold-open wall time.

Refactor to parse the CREATE TABLE string at most once per row,
then hand the same CreateTableStatement to every helper. Concretely:

- crates/fsqlite-core/src/compat_persist.rs splits each string-entry
  helper into a pair: the public `…_from_sql(sql)` variant keeps
  its existing signature for callers who only have raw SQL, and a
  new pub(crate) `…_from_create_table_statement(create)` variant
  takes the already-parsed AST and does the same work without
  re-tokenizing. The _from_sql variants now delegate to their AST
  counterparts after parse_single_statement + pattern-matching,
  and only fall back to the legacy tokenizer path when parsing
  fails. Specifically:
    * is_autoincrement_table_sql → autoincrement_from_create_table_statement
    * extract_check_constraints_from_sql → check_constraints_from_create_table_statement
    * try_parse_columns_from_create_sql_ast → columns_from_create_table_statement

- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs: the schema-reload hot loop
  in Connection::load_schema_from_sqlite_master() now computes a
  single `parsed_create_table = parse_single_statement(&create_sql)`
  and reuses it for:
    * columns_from_create_table_statement (with fallback to the
      string-entry path for rows the parser rejects)
    * without_rowid — taken directly from create.without_rowid
      when the parse succeeded
    * is_autoincrement — via autoincrement_from_create_table_statement
    * strict — taken directly from create.strict
    * check_defs — via check_constraints_from_create_table_statement
    * FK + PRIMARY KEY + table-level UNIQUE walks — the existing
      `if let Ok(Statement::CreateTable(..))` block becomes
      `if let Some(create_stmt) = parsed_create_table.as_ref()`,
      consuming the pre-parsed AST. All downstream `ref foo` bindings
      swap to `&` pattern matching so the borrow shapes still
      compile against the now-borrowed create_stmt.

Every fallback path still exists: if parse_single_statement returns
an error or yields a non-CreateTable statement, parsed_create_table
is None and each helper invocation takes the legacy …_from_sql
entry point, so behaviour is unchanged for legacy / malformed rows.

Adds an ignored manual perf probe
`manual_perf_probe_connection_open_schema_reload_many_tables`
under pager_routing_tests that builds a 32-table fixture (each
with AUTOINCREMENT, FK self-reference, UNIQUE + CHECK constraints,
STRICT, a secondary index — i.e. a full slate of parse work per
row), then runs Connection::open + a trivial count query
FSQLITE_SCHEMA_REOPEN_ITERS times (default 120) and prints
p50_us / p95_us. Gated behind #[ignore] so CI doesn't run it;
reachable via `cargo test --release -- --ignored
manual_perf_probe_connection_open_schema_reload_many_tables`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
